{{Quote|Therein lies the future of the galaxy.|[[Jorus C'baoth]]|<ref name="OBF" />}}
'''''Outbound Flight''''' was the main [[Starship/Legends|ship]] of the [[Outbound Flight Project]]. It consisted of a [[Central Storage Core|central fuselage]] surrounded by six [[Dreadnaught-class heavy cruiser/Legends|''Dreadnaught''-class heavy cruisers]] designated [[Dreadnaught-One|D-One]] through [[Dreadnaught-Six|D-Six]]; together, the massive ship was known as ''Outbound Flight''.

==Description==
The [[Central Storage Core]] was the main hub of ''Outbound Flight'', and contained the food and supplies that the [[Colonist/Legends|colonists]] would need on their journey. The core was surrounded by six [[Dreadnaught-class heavy cruiser/Legends|''Dreadnaught''-class heavy cruisers]] arranged with the storage capacity so that if any of the six Dreadnaughts detached, they could each easily load one-sixth of its supplies into their ship. In fact, it was planned that one ''Dreadnaught'' would be left behind for defense in the event that a colony was established.<ref name="OBF" /> On each Dreadnaught was a dining room or cafeteria in which the colonists ate their meals. There was talk of decorating each of the different cafeterias with themes from different parts of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], but the request was turned down.<ref name="OBF" />

The Combined Operations Center or ComOps Center for ''Outbound Flight'' was located on Dreadnaught-One just behind the [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]]/[[Computer monitor/Legends|monitor]] room complex, stretching for thirty [[Meter/Legends|meters]] between the two main bow corridors. From this area, [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Pakmillu]] had control over all six Dreadnaughts. All 200 single-seat [[Landspeeder/Legends|landspeeders]] and [[Swoop/Legends|swoops]] used for transportation around ''Outbound Flight'' could be controlled from this point.<ref name="OBF" />

The configuration of the Dreadnaughts gave ''Outbound Flight'' a very formidable armament of [[Quad laser cannon/Legends|quad laser cannons]], [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]], and [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolasers]]. The 50,000 passengers were housed in a mobile [[Space station/Legends|space station]] composed of the central fuselage.

==History==
{{Quote|Hego Damask is one of those beings. It was Hego Damask who was responsible for overseeing development of the Republic Reserve Administration and for providing financial support for the Resettlement Acts that enabled beings to blaze new hyperspace routes to the outlying systems and colonize distant worlds.|Darth Sidious|<ref name="Darth Plagueis (novel)">[[Darth Plagueis (novel)|''Darth Plagueis'']]</ref>}}
Preparation began in [[29 BBY/Legends|29 BBY]] and the expedition departed from [[Yaga Minor/Legends|Yaga Minor]] in [[27 BBY/Legends|27 BBY]]. While nominally commanded by Captain Pakmillu, [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Jorus C'baoth]] quickly assumed more and more control over the operations of ''Outbound Flight''. He recruited the children of the colonists, who were chosen for probable Force-sensitivity, to be new Jedi trainees, ignoring the wishes of their parents. This was part of his plan to create a new society where Jedi would directly guide and govern non-Jedi. He also began to unilaterally pass judgment over disputes between non-Jedi, even when mediation was not asked for. This began to cause discontent in the civilian population, especially the technician [[Chas Uliar]]. [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] questioned C'baoth's methods, warning that trying to rule non-Jedi could lead to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]]. However, Kenobi and his [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] were removed from Outbound Flight before they left Republic space because of [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], who as the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] Darth Sidious wished to someday train Skywalker as his [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]].<ref name="OBF" />

[[File:OutboundFlight-SotGSE.png|thumb|left|250px|Core with six Dreadnaughts]]
Sidious had sent [[Special Task Force One]] to destroy ''Outbound Flight'', but that was devastated by a [[Chiss Expansionary Defense Force|CEDF]] force led by the young [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Mitth'raw'nuruodo]], who would later become the infamous [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] Thrawn. [[Kinman Doriana/Legends|Kinman Doriana]], the leader of the task force, was spared, and convinced Thrawn to take on the task of eliminating ''Outbound Flight''.<ref name="OBF" />

Thrawn used a [[Gravity well projector/Legends|gravity well projector]] to force Outbound Flight out of hyperspace. He decided to try negotiation first, and met with C'baoth and his former Padawan, [[Lorana Jinzler]], aboard ''Outbound Flight''. However C'baoth refused to listen to Thrawn, especially when he mentioned the name of Darth Sidious. Chas Uliar attempted to talk to Thrawn while he was escorted off the ship in order to get his help in overthrowing C'baoth's tyranny. He was caught by C'baoth himself, who had Jinzler imprison him and fifty-six other colonists who shared similar views in the central storage core.<ref name="OBF" />

After Thrawn returned to his own ship, the [[Vagaari/Legends|Vagaari]] arrived due to Thrawn's manipulations with the help of the smuggler [[Jorj Car'das]]. As the Vagaari fleet stormed towards ''Outbound Flight'', C'baoth performed a [[Force-meld|Force meld]] to [[Mind trick/Legends|trick]] the enemy. At that moment Thrawn launched [[Vulture-class starfighter/Legends|droid starfighters]] to engage the confused Vagaari, creating a large [[Wound in the Force/Legends|disturbance in the Force]] which overwhelmed the meld participants. During the disturbance, C'baoth and his fellow Jedi failed to detect the Chiss forces that were sneaking in, and Thrawn managed to systematically destroy the weapon clusters of ''Outbound Flight'', killing almost every Jedi aboard. At this point, Thrawn gave Outbound Flight another chance to turn back and contacted C'baoth. Enraged, Jorus gave into his anger and succumbed to the dark side and [[Telekinesis/Legends#Force Choke|Force-choked]] Thrawn across space from ''Outbound Flight's'' bridge itself, almost killing him. However, before Thrawn could die, Doriana activated the secondary command layer pre-set in the droid starfighters, which then rammed and blasted open the ''Outbound Flight''. The final wave of starfighters employed a [[radiation bomb]] of Chiss design on the ship, killing C'baoth and most of the passengers aboard.<ref name="OBF" />

Only Lorana Jinzler and the fifty-seven colonists imprisoned in the core managed to survive the [[Bomb/Legends|bombs]]. The Chiss began to take control of the ship, but were recalled by [[Aristocra/Legends|Aristocra]] [[Chaf'orm'bintrano]], who wished to take the ship for his own family. Only [[Syndic/Legends|Syndic]] [[Mitth'ras'safis/Legends|Mitth'ras'safis]] (Thrass), of equal rank to Chaf'orm'bintrano, stayed on the ship. Lorana met with Thrass and they formed a plan to hide ''Outbound Flight'' in [[The Redoubt/Legends|The Redoubt]] where the colonists would be safe until Thrass could argue their case to the [[Council of Families]] of the [[Chiss Ascendancy/Legends|Chiss Ascendancy]]. However the ship's hyperdrive soon failed, and they had to crash-land the craft on a small [[Unidentified planetoid (The Redoubt)|planetoid]]. A [[Stone (disambiguation)|stone]]-filled [[Unidentified valley/Legends|valley]] was chosen to absorb the impact of landing. A few moments before impact, Lorana and Thrass realized the fifty-seven survivors had moved from the central storage core to [[Dreadnaught-Four|D-4]], the Dreadnaught on the opposite side of D-1, their location. Lorana and Thrass knew they would have to land on D-1 to save D-4, and gave their lives so that the remaining colonists would have a chance for survival.<ref name="OBF" />

Before leaving the Republic, genetic samples were taken from all passengers and crew, with the stated intention of preventing a [[Hive virus]].<ref name="OBF" /> C'baoth's sample was used by Emperor Palpatine to create a clone of him, called [[Joruus C'baoth]]. This clone was discovered in [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]],<ref name="The New Essential Chronology">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> by Thrawn. The clone turned out even more [[Clone madness|unstable]] than the original donor, and eventually attempted to take control of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] from Thrawn.<ref name="Dark Force Rising">''[[Dark Force Rising]]''</ref> The clone was ultimately killed in a [[Assault on Mount Tantiss|confrontation]] with Jedi Master [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and the former [[Emperor's Hand/Legends|Emperor's Hand]] [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]].<ref name="The Last Command">''[[The Last Command]]''</ref>

In [[22 ABY/Legends|22 ABY]], a Chiss [[Unidentified Chiss patrol|patrol]] discovered the remains of ''Outbound Flight'' and its support ships fifty years after its destruction. The survivors of the project had founded a meager, self-sufficient colony on the planet that Lorana and Thrass landed them on. However, the Vagaari (disguised as peaceful [[Geroon (species)|Geroon]]) attempted to destroy the surviving colonists and the Chiss along with their [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], and [[Empire of the Hand]] allies during the [[Battle of the Redoubt]]. They succeeded in detaching a Dreadnaught from the wreckage, although they were foiled by a [[Delta-12 Skysprite-class|Delta-12 ''Skysprite'']] and the Chiss fleet.<ref name="SQ">''[[Survivor's Quest]]''</ref>
